column1: When is the best time to sow sugarcane?
column2: The ideal time to sow sugarcane is between February and April. This ensures optimal growth conditions for the crop.

column1: What should I do about worms in my gourd plants?
column2: To control worms in gourd plants, spray a neem-based biopesticide like azadirachtin at 5 ml per liter of water. Apply in the early morning or late afternoon for best results, and repeat every 7-10 days if needed.

column1: Why are my okra leaves turning yellow?
column2: Yellowing okra leaves may indicate a fungal issue. Apply mancozeb at 2 grams per liter of water as a foliar spray. Ensure proper drainage and avoid overwatering to prevent further issues.

column1: How do I control pests in my coriander crop?
column2: For pest control in coriander, use a neem oil solution at 5 ml per liter of water. Spray evenly on the plants, focusing on the undersides of leaves, and repeat every 10 days. Always follow safety guidelines when using pesticides.

column1: Why are my tomato leaves turning black?
column2: Black tomato leaves are often caused by fungal diseases like blight. Spray mancozeb at 2 grams per liter of water every 7-10 days. Remove and destroy affected leaves to prevent the spread.

column1: What's the ideal plant-to-plant distance for crops?
column2: The recommended plant-to-plant distance varies by crop, but for most vegetables, maintain 10-12 meters between rows and 10-12 meters between plants within a row. Check specific crop guidelines for precision.

column1: How should I treat groundnut seeds before planting?
column2: Treat groundnut seeds with thiram at 2.5 grams per kg of seeds to protect against fungal diseases. Mix thoroughly and sow immediately for best results.

column1: How can I control weeds in my maize field?
column2: To control weeds in maize, apply atrazine 50 WP at 2 liters per 800 liters of water as a pre-emergence spray within 2-3 days of sowing. Follow up with manual weeding if needed.

column1: What should I do about rust in my wheat crop?
column2: To manage wheat rust, spray propiconazole at 500 ml per hectare in 700-800 liters of water. Apply at the first sign of rust and repeat after 15 days if necessary.

column1: When and how much ginger seed should I sow?
column2: Sow ginger in April-May using 15-18 quintals of seed per hectare. Ensure well-drained soil and treat seeds with carbendazim at 2 grams per liter of water to prevent rot.

column1: Which pea varieties are good for growing with lentils?
column2: Recommended pea varieties for intercropping with lentils include Rachna, Hans Pusa-10, and BR-2. These varieties are compatible and yield well in mixed cropping systems.

column1: How do I handle pest attacks on my pumpkin crop?
column2: For pest control in pumpkins, use a neem-based biopesticide like azadirachtin at 5 ml per liter of water. Spray every 7-10 days, targeting the undersides of leaves.

column1: What's the best way to manage pests in my okra crop?
column2: To manage pests in okra, spray imidacloprid at 0.5 ml per liter of water. Apply in the early morning and repeat every 10-14 days. Always wear protective gear during application.

column1: How can I treat blight in my tomato crop?
column2: To control tomato blight, apply mancozeb at 2 grams per liter of water mixed with streptocycline at 1 gram per liter. Spray every 7-10 days and remove affected plant parts.

column1: What are some common fish varieties for farming?
column2: Popular fish varieties for farming include Rohu, Catla, Nain, Common Carp, and Silver Carp. These are hardy and suitable for pond aquaculture.

column1: What are the different types of ginger?
column2: Common ginger varieties include Suprabha, Suruchi, and Surabhi. These are high-yielding and resistant to common diseases.

column1: How can I get a credit card?
column2: For information on credit cards, please contact your nearest bank manager. They can guide you through the application process and eligibility criteria.

column1: How do I treat onion smut disease?
column2: To prevent onion smut, treat onion seeds with captan or thiram at 2.5 grams per kg of seeds before sowing. This protects against fungal infections.
